finding: The composite signal engine loses to buy-and-hold as a timing tool: 2020-2025 strategy +250.9% vs B&H +740.0%, Sharpe 0.675 vs 0.921, sell-signal 3M hit rate 28.6% (target was 55%).
effect_size: -489pp total return vs B&H over 66 monthly signals
lesson: Aegis is a risk-awareness tool, not a timing tool. Sell signals fire at high-VIX moments that are historically the best buying opportunities. Do not propose timing overlays on the signal engine.

finding: 12-month crash prediction has no skill: Brier at the 12m horizon is indistinguishable from the climatological base rate, and lagging indicators dominate its SHAP.
lesson: Long-horizon crash probabilities are decoration. Only the 3m horizon ever showed tentative skill, and it rests on ~7 stress events.

finding: LPPLS (Sornette log-periodic) predictive skill was adversarially refuted twice; it ships as a descriptive bubble-structure flag only.
lesson: Never propose LPPLS as a timing input or lane trigger; it is descriptive context.

finding: A survivorship-free backtest universe is NOT buildable on free data: of 20 real delisted S&P names, yfinance returns 15 nothing, 4 wrong-company-on-recycled-symbol, 1 usable (5%).
lesson: No backtested absolute-alpha number on free data is trustworthy, and DSR/PBO cannot detect the bias (it guards multiple-testing, not a biased universe). Selection signals validate FORWARD (PIT IC + lane NAV) until a survivorship-free feed (Sharadar planned) lands.

finding: The insider collector passed 12 offline tests and failed 100% of its prod SEC fetches (403 on every www.sec.gov/Archives call) because it bypassed the process-wide rate limiter; the IC clock looked alive while accruing nothing.
effect_size: 100% prod fetch failure while all tests green
lesson: A collector that runs but fetches nothing reads as covered on every dashboard. All SEC calls go through one paced choke point (_sec_get, <=8/s, declared UA); every new collector gets a live prod check, not just tests.

finding: The M3 crash-model retrain works end-to-end and still proves nothing: binary >=20%-drawdown label collapses to a handful of independent episodes; walk-forward AUC unmeasurable (zero events in purged validation windows); outputs near-constant.
effect_size: AUC unmeasurable; 6m head emits one value; 12m headline ~3x base rate with no discrimination
lesson: Rare-event binary labels on one market's history cannot support learning. An honest model_not_deployed beats a skill-less number lighting the overlay.

finding: The severity/exceedance successor (TRIAL-CRASH-2, pre-registered before first fit) ALSO failed its gate: 0/6 dense cells, negative Brier skill vs climatology everywhere (5% cells -0.32 to -0.54, systematic over-prediction out-of-distribution); STLFSI4 itself barely beats climatology.
effect_size: 0/6 dense cells; skill vs climatology -0.005 to -0.54
lesson: Third consecutive confirmation of canon A5. Honest nuance: 10%-threshold cells show real RANKING signal (PR-AUC up to ~3.6x prevalence at 30d) with unusable calibration - a calibration-on-ranker attempt is a legitimate NEW trial (TRIAL-CRASH-3), never a rerun.

finding: Canon A5, thrice-confirmed: short-horizon crash-TIMING skill on free market/macro features is approximately zero. The fragility composite (descriptive, currently the crisis read) is the honest replacement.
lesson: Do not propose crash-timing models, features, or overlays as improvement hypotheses. Measure fragility; scale exposure; never time.

finding: The field converged on OUR protocol in 2025-26: leakage-controlled LIVE evaluations show frontier LLMs overwhelmingly lose money trading (DeepFund live month: 8 of 9 flagship LLMs net-negative, only Grok 3 mini positive; Alpha Arena real-money S1: GPT-5 -62.7%, Gemini 2.5 Pro -56.7%, Claude Sonnet 4.5 -30.8%; only DeepSeek and Qwen3 Max positive); of 19 LLM-trading studies only 2 had time-consistent splits and 15/19 were irreproducible. Nobody surveyed runs pre-registered paper-lane forward records.
effect_size: Alpha Arena S1 real money: GPT-5 -62.7%, Gemini -56.7%, Claude -30.8%; DeepFund 8/9 negative
lesson: The forward record + pre-registration IS the product moat. Absorb components (ratchets, memory, verification infra), never LLM-as-trader.

finding: Canon A2: LLM/brain stock picks are never backtested - GPT-4o retrieves in-training-window S&P 500 closes to <1% error while post-cutoff MAPE is ~13-20%, so published in-window LLM backtest alpha is often literal memorization (profit mirage). Masking/pretend-you-don't-know prompts fail to prevent recall.
effect_size: <1% price recall error inside training window
evidence: Lopez-Lira, Tang & Zhu, arXiv:2504.14765 'The Memorization Problem' (citation corrected 2026-07-16; was misattributed to 2605.24564)
lesson: The conviction lane (forward, logged decisions) is the ONLY honest test of discretionary/LLM-adjacent picks. No LLM output touches a trade path.

finding: The lanes flipped in five weeks: conviction went +5.5% -> -5.4% while mirror went -8.4% -> +0.79% - a ~14-point ordering reversal on the same book.
lesson: Every short-window 'we are winning' read is noise; the 24-month bar is justified by our own data, not by cited papers. Never let an interim lane ranking drive a strategy change.

finding: A refactor deleted a variable but left five references; every per-ticker analysis raised NameError post-MC for ~2 days, disclosed only as 'screener skip' warnings, while deploy verification exercised the NEW endpoints instead of the CHANGED module. Fixing it un-masked a second latent bug (numpy arrays in the screener response that had only ever serialized by a mutation race).
effect_size: screener + stock pages 100% dead ~2 days; all green dashboards
lesson: Verify the changed surface with a cache-busted live request; grep deleted variables; read the prod warning stream; optional-field extractors get broad excepts (degrade per-field), response payloads never carry internal numpy objects.

finding: Vol-managed momentum printed a false PASS through the full DSR/PBO gate because the universe itself was survivor-biased - the deflation guards measure multiple-testing, not data bias.
lesson: Statistical guards do not sanctify biased inputs. Data integrity checks precede deflation checks.

finding: Seven forward measurements are accruing and none may be read early: 6 forward-IC clocks (insider T9, revisions T10, multifactor T8, quality, PEAD, congress/ARK) since 2026-06/07, and TRIAL-FORECAST-LEDGER (MC-implied 1y vs street target-implied 1y, paired MAE + bias at 12m maturity) since 2026-07-16 with earliest decision 2027-07-16.
lesson: Propose NO new signals: six clocks is the cap - each new one raises the multiple-testing bar for all. Improvement hypotheses should target calibration, cost, data quality, UX, or verification instead.

finding: Yahoo 429 storms were surfacing as 404 'Could not analyze' because per-service duplicate yf.Ticker calls hit rate limits independently; one shared per-ticker fetch (single 10y history sliced per period, 90s breaker, stale-serve <=24h, RateLimited -> 503 + Retry-After) fixed the class.
lesson: Fetch once per entity per window and share; rate limiting is disclosed as 503, never mislabeled as missing data. GDELT/others follow the same pattern: a failed fetch is disclosed, never defaulted to a calm reading.
